title: 3 things the Bruins must fix if they want to punch their ticket to the playoffs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins' power play struggles, dropping from third to 28th in the NHL since the Olympic break.
Morgan Geekie and Elias Lindholm's third-line performance is weak, contributing zero points at 5-on-5 play over 11 games.
Andrew Peeke and Nikita Zadorov have been outscored 4-2 since returning from the Olympics, causing third-pairing instability.

title: 5 Red Sox takeaways as Wilyer Abreu and Eduardo Rodriguez lift Venezuela to World Baseball Classic title
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: Wilyer Abreu hit key home runs, helping Venezuela win their first World Baseball Classic title.
Former Red Sox pitcher Eduardo Rodriguez delivered scoreless innings in the championship game for Venezuela.
Garrett Whitlock allowed the decisive run, while Roman Anthony struck out to end the game for Team USA.

title: This Ashland mom is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for children with complex medical needs and their parents
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Mengyuan Flanagan from Ashland, Mass., is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Michael Lisnow Respite Center.
The Michael Lisnow Respite Center supports children with complex medical conditions and offers relief to their families.
Flanagan has volunteered at the center and aims to raise funds while running the marathon.

title: Western Mass. couple found guilty of manslaughter in death of foster child
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Tucker and Cassandra Barlow-Tucker convicted of involuntary manslaughter for the death of their 10-month-old foster child.
The child died in February 2020 from untreated complications of a strep infection, bronchopneumonia, and empyema.
The couple will be sentenced on March 19, facing up to 20 years for manslaughter and 2.5 years for endangerment.

title: Major water main break in Jackson Square floods streets, causes detours
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A 36-inch water main broke at Columbus Avenue, Centre, and Ritchie streets, flooding Jackson Square on Tuesday.
Boston police rerouted traffic, while MBTA detoured bus routes 14, 22, 29, 41, and 44 to avoid the area.
Crews partially shut down the water main by 5:40 p.m.; efforts to drain streets continue into the night.

title: Why Jaylen Waddle’s trade to Broncos is bad news for the Patriots
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Miami Dolphins traded Jaylen Waddle and a fourth-round pick to the Denver Broncos for three draft picks, including a first-rounder.
The Waddle trade increases pressure on the Patriots, who may need to offer a first-round pick for Eagles WR A.J. Brown.
The Broncos improve their roster with Waddle, posing a stronger challenge to AFC teams, including the New England Patriots.

title: 2 charged after car hits pedestrians outside of downtown Boston nightclub
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Darius Boyce, 45, and Maria Moraes, 25, charged after hitting three pedestrians with a Jeep outside ICON nightclub in Boston.
Boyce faces multiple charges including felony assault; Moraes charged with interfering with police and disorderly conduct.
Victims identified Boyce and Moraes; police found a loaded firearm in the car during a stop shortly after the incident.

title: Former President Biden makes surprise St. Patrick’s Day appearance in Boston
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Former President Joe Biden made a surprise appearance at the Irish American Partnership’s St. Patrick’s Day Breakfast in Boston.
Biden's speech emphasized his Irish ancestors' immigration experiences and criticized current immigration policies under President Trump.
Governor Maura Healey shared remarks alongside Biden, highlighting Massachusetts-Ireland connections and addressing immigrant challenges.

title: 2 Boston College students injured in deck collapse during off-campus party
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two Boston College students were injured after a deck railing collapsed at a party on 64 Undine Road, Brighton.
Railing failure caused the students to fall from the second floor, with both landing on heads and spines.
Investigation revealed rotting wood and short corroded nails, possibly concealed by paint, as factors in the collapse.

title: Patriots rookie earns hefty performance bonus after strong 2025 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Craig Woodson, a Patriots rookie, led the defense with 950 snaps and started 15 games in 2025.
Woodson earned a $1,149,910 performance bonus on top of his $840,000 base salary for 2025.
Woodson had 79 tackles and excelled in the postseason, including 10 tackles in Super Bowl LX.
